mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor markets historically correlate with equity performance.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals a weakening economy and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate profits decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes economic downturns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crosses above 5.5% and S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with moderate inflation indicates healthy consumer demand without overheating, fostering an environment conducive to corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL inflation conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital and discount future earnings more aggressively, leading to downward pressure on equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by 0.5% or more over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates increase borrowing costs for companies.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS shows a sustained increase as described, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive) over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signifies strong manufacturing activity and business investment, indicating healthy economic expansion that supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth meets the trigger condition, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 1-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A consistently high level of housing starts reflects strong demand in the housing sector and broader economic confidence, which typically translates into increased construction activity and consumer spending, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If HOUST remains at high levels (e.g., above 1.5 million units annualized) but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative over a 1-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth coupled with a low unemployment rate signifies a strong and stable economy, where businesses are expanding and consumers are employed, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and unemployment are primary economic health indicators.
failure_condition: If conditions of strong GDP growth and low unemployment are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is persistently high, it pressures the central bank to raise interest rates, which can dampen economic activity and reduce investor appetite for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS has increased in the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation often leads to central bank tightening.
failure_condition: If high inflation and recent FEDFUNDS increases are observed, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 1-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
